subtasks.description:
Create src/tools/Workspace.mag — a scratch pad window with basic text editing.

Create the directory src/tools/ if it does not exist.

File: src/tools/Workspace.mag
Namespace: Alto, Import: Go.

Workspace subclass: View
  instanceVars: textLines cursorX cursorY scrollOffset

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:

1. method: initialize
   - Call super initialize.
   - textLines := Array new: 0.  (NOT Array new)
   - cursorX := 0.
   - cursorY := 0.
   - scrollOffset := 0.

2. classMethod: open: aDisplay
   - Create a Rectangle frame (e.g., x: 1 y: 1 width: 60 height: 20).
   - Create a Workspace instance (ws := Workspace new).
   - Create a Window: Window title: 'Workspace' content: ws controller: nil frame: frame.
     (Pass nil for controller since Workspace handles its own events — OR create a simple Controller that delegates to the Workspace view.)
   - Actually: Window takes content: aView controller: aController. The event loop in Display>>run dispatches to (windows at: windows size - 1) handleEvent: event, which goes to Window>>handleEvent: which delegates to contentController. So either:
     a) Make Workspace also be the contentController (but it's a View subclass, not Controller), or
     b) Create a thin Controller subclass that delegates handleEvent: to the Workspace view.
     The simplest approach: create the Window with controller set to the Workspace itself. Window>>handleEvent: just calls contentController handleEvent: evt. So Workspace needs to respond to handleEvent:.
     Since View already has no handleEvent: method, Workspace can implement handleEvent: directly.
     Set the Window's contentController to the workspace instance.
   - Call aDisplay addWindow: window.
   - Return the workspace (or window).
   ALL temp vars declared at method top. No mid-block | var | declarations.

3. method: handleEvent: evt
   Temp vars at top: | key ch lineStr leftPart rightPart newLine visibleHeight |
   - Extract key from evt (evt is a tcell event object — use Go::Terminal event API).
   - NOTE: Look at how Display>>run polls events: event := screen pollEvent. The event object is a Go tcell event. Check its type. For key events, use Go::Terminal event methods. The exact API depends on Maggie's Go FFI. Use pattern: check if event responds to key/rune methods.
   - For key events, handle:
     a) Escape (key code 27): close the window. Call the parent window's close method or call the display's removeWindow:. Simplest: the Workspace needs a reference to its window or display. Add an instance var 'window' or 'display' to track this. OR: just return a symbol like #close that Window>>handleEvent: checks.
        REVISED approach: Add instance var 'parentWindow' to Workspace. In open:, set ws parentWindow: window. In handleEvent:, on Escape, call parentWindow close. But Window>>close is a stub (^self). We need to also remove from display. Add instance var 'display' to Workspace. On Escape: display removeWindow: parentWindow. display stop (if no windows left).
        SIMPLEST approach: Add instanceVars: textLines cursorX cursorY scrollOffset display parentWindow.
        On Escape: display removeWindow: parentWindow.
     b) Enter (key code 13): Insert a new line.
        - Split current line at cursorX: leftPart := (textLines at: cursorY) copyFrom: 0 to: cursorX. rightPart := (textLines at: cursorY) copyFrom: cursorX to: end.
        - Actually, use String slicing. Maggie strings likely support copyFrom:to: or similar. Use substring operations.
        - Replace textLines at cursorY with leftPart.
        - Insert rightPart after cursorY. Since we can't use add:/insert:, rebuild the array:
          Build new array: elements 0..cursorY are same, then rightPart, then cursorY+1..end.
          Use a loop or collect pattern.
        - cursorY := cursorY + 1. cursorX := 0.
     c) Backspace (key code 8 or 127):
        - If cursorX > 0: remove char at cursorX-1 from current line. cursorX := cursorX - 1.
        - If cursorX = 0 and cursorY > 0: merge current line onto end of previous line.
          Set cursorX to previous line's length. Concatenate. Remove current line from array using reject: or rebuild.
     d) Arrow keys: Up (cursorY := (cursorY - 1) max: 0), Down (cursorY := (cursorY + 1) min: textLines size - 1), Left (cursorX := (cursorX - 1) max: 0), Right (cursorX := (cursorX + 1) min: current line size).
     e) Printable chars (char code 32-126): Insert character at cursorX in current line. Rebuild string. cursorX := cursorX + 1.
   - After any edit, call self invalidate.
   - Handle scrollOffset: if cursorY < scrollOffset, scrollOffset := cursorY. If cursorY >= scrollOffset + visible height, scrollOffset := cursorY - visible height + 1.

   IMPORTANT: Use integer char codes, not dollar-sign literals. E.g., 27 not $ESC, 13 not $CR, 8 not $BS.
   ALL temp vars at the TOP of the method. No mid-block | var | declarations.
   Use isNil ifTrue: not ifNil:. Use Array new: 0 not Array new. Use copyWith: not add:. Use reject: not remove:.

4. method: displayOn: aCellBuffer
   Temp vars at top: | visibleHeight i lineIndex lineStr j ch charCode reverseStyle |
   - visibleHeight := aCellBuffer height.
   - Loop i from 0 to visibleHeight - 1:
     lineIndex := scrollOffset + i.
     If lineIndex < textLines size:
       lineStr := textLines at: lineIndex.  (Use 0-based indexing if Array is 0-based — but check: CellBuffer uses 1-based Array internally with +1 offset. Model uses copyWith:/reject:. ListView uses items at: itemIndex + 1 — BUT that was flagged as a bug! Check: Array>>at: appears to be 1-based in this codebase based on CellBuffer's cells at: idx where idx = y*width+x+1. So Array is 1-BASED.)
       IMPORTANT: Arrays are 1-BASED in this codebase. CellBuffer indexX:y: returns y*width+x+1. So textLines at: lineIndex + 1 for 0-based lineIndex.
       Loop j from 0 to lineStr size - 1 (or use 1-based string indexing):
         If lineIndex = cursorY and j = cursorX:
           Render with reverse-video style (Dictionary with #attrs -> #reverse).
         Else:
           Render normally.
         charCode := (lineStr at: j + 1) value.  (1-based string, .value for char code — but CellBuffer putString uses charCode. Check: line 66 of CellBuffer: (str at: i) charCode. So strings are 1-based and use charCode method.)
         aCellBuffer putChar: charCode x: j y: i style: style.
     If lineIndex = cursorY and cursorX >= lineStr size (cursor past end of line):
       Render a space with reverse-video at cursorX, i.
   - If textLines size = 0 (empty buffer), render cursor at 0,0 with reverse-video space.

5. classMethod: new
   Standard pattern:
   | instance |
   instance := super new.
   instance initialize.
   ^instance

6. method: parentWindow: aWindow [ parentWindow := aWindow ]
7. method: display: aDisplay [ display := aDisplay ]

CONVENTIONS:
- namespace: 'Alto', import: 'Go' at top of file.
- Integer char codes: 27 (Escape), 13 (Enter), 8 (Backspace), 32 (Space).
- ALL temp vars at top of method in single | ... | declaration.
- No ifNil:/ifNotNil: — use isNil ifTrue: / notNil ifTrue:.
- No mid-block | var | declarations.
- Use Array new: 0 not Array new.
- Use copyWith: not add:. Use reject: not remove:.
- Use at:ifAbsent: with ifAbsent block where needed.
- Arrays are 1-based. Strings are 1-based with charCode method.
- CellBuffer coordinates are 0-based.
- Follow the same class definition style as Window.mag, Label.mag, ListView.mag.

REFERENCE FILES:
- src/widgets/Window.mag (Window structure, displayOn:, handleEvent:)
- src/widgets/ListView.mag (event handling pattern, displayOn: with styles)
- src/mvc/View.mag (base class, invalidate, buffer, bounds)
- src/display/CellBuffer.mag (putChar:x:y:style:, putString:x:y:style:, clear, width, height)
- src/display/Display.mag (addWindow:, removeWindow:, run event loop)
- src/display/Rectangle.mag (x:y:width:height: constructor)

Create src/tools/Transcript.mag — a read-only scrolling log window, singleton.

Create the directory src/tools/ if it does not exist.

File: src/tools/Transcript.mag
Namespace: Alto, Import: Go.

Transcript subclass: View
  instanceVars: lines maxLines scrollOffset display parentWindow

Class-side singleton var: Use a class variable or class-side pattern. In Maggie/Smalltalk style, use a class-side instance var. Simplest: use a classMethod that stores in a class-level variable.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:

1. method: initialize
   - Call super initialize.
   - lines := Array new: 0.  (NOT Array new)
   - maxLines := 1000.
   - scrollOffset := 0.
   - display := nil.
   - parentWindow := nil.

2. classMethod: current
   - Singleton accessor. Pattern: check if a class-side variable 'Current' is set.
   - In Maggie, class variables may use: Transcript current checks/sets a class-side holder.
   - Implementation: Use a class variable or a simple pattern:
     classMethod: current [
       Current isNil ifTrue: [Current := self new].
       ^Current
     ]
   - NOTE: Check if Maggie supports class variables. If not, use a global or a class-side instance variable pattern. Look at how other classes handle singletons — there are none in the existing codebase, so use the simplest pattern that works.
   - If class variables aren't supported, an alternative: store the singleton reference as a class-side method that caches. Or use: classMethod: current [ ^Current ] and classMethod: current: anInstance [ Current := anInstance ] with the first call to open: setting it.

3. classMethod: show: aString
   - Convenience method to append a line to the singleton.
   - self current appendLine: aString.

4. classMethod: open: aDisplay
   - Create a Rectangle frame (e.g., x: 1 y: 22 width: 60 height: 10 — positioned below the Workspace).
   - Get or create the singleton: transcript := self current.
   - transcript display: aDisplay.
   - Create a Window: Window title: 'Transcript' content: transcript controller: transcript frame: frame.
   - transcript parentWindow: window.
   - aDisplay addWindow: window.
   - Return transcript.
   ALL temp vars at method top.

5. method: appendLine: aString
   Temp vars at top: | newLines |
   - lines := lines copyWith: aString.
   - If lines size > maxLines, trim oldest:
     lines := lines copyFrom: (lines size - maxLines + 1) to: lines size.
     (Or rebuild array keeping last maxLines entries.)
   - scrollOffset := (lines size - visibleHeight) max: 0.  (auto-scroll to bottom)
     Actually, we may not know visibleHeight here. Set scrollOffset to lines size (will be clamped in displayOn:). Or: scrollOffset := lines size. Then in displayOn:, compute visible window.
     Simplest: scrollOffset := (lines size - 1) max: 0. The displayOn: method will show lines from scrollOffset backwards/forwards.
   - self invalidate.

6. method: handleEvent: evt
   Temp vars at top: | key visibleHeight |
   - Extract key from event.
   - Up arrow: scrollOffset := (scrollOffset - 1) max: 0. self invalidate.
   - Down arrow: scrollOffset := (scrollOffset + 1) min: ((lines size - 1) max: 0). self invalidate.
   - Escape: display removeWindow: parentWindow.
   - Ignore all other keys (read-only).
   Use integer char codes. ALL temp vars at top. No ifNil:/ifNotNil:.

7. method: displayOn: aCellBuffer
   Temp vars at top: | visibleHeight i lineIndex lineStr |
   - Rendering: show lines from scrollOffset, newest at bottom.
   - visibleHeight := aCellBuffer height.
   - Compute start: we want to show 'visibleHeight' lines ending near scrollOffset position.
   - Simple approach: show lines starting from scrollOffset, going down.
     i := 0.
     [i < visibleHeight] whileTrue: [
       lineIndex := scrollOffset + i.
       (lineIndex < lines size) ifTrue: [
         lineStr := lines at: lineIndex + 1.  (1-based array)
         aCellBuffer putString: lineStr x: 0 y: i style: nil.
       ].
       i := i + 1
     ].
   - For 'newest at bottom' behavior: when auto-scrolling (appendLine:), set scrollOffset so that the last line appears at the bottom:
     scrollOffset := (lines size - visibleHeight) max: 0.

8. method: parentWindow: aWindow [ parentWindow := aWindow ]
9. method: display: aDisplay [ display := aDisplay ]
10. method: lines [ ^lines ]
11. method: maxLines [ ^maxLines ]
12. method: scrollOffset [ ^scrollOffset ]

13. classMethod: new
    Standard pattern:
    | instance |
    instance := super new.
    instance initialize.
    ^instance

SINGLETON PATTERN:
- The safest approach in Maggie: use a class variable 'Current' initialized to nil.
  classMethod: current [
    Current isNil ifTrue: [Current := self new].
    ^Current
  ]
- If Maggie doesn't support class variables in this syntax, use an alternative like storing it in a well-known global.
- The open: method should use self current to get/create the singleton, NOT self new.

CONVENTIONS:
- namespace: 'Alto', import: 'Go' at top of file.
- Integer char codes for event handling.
- ALL temp vars at top of method in single | ... | declaration.
- No ifNil:/ifNotNil: — use isNil ifTrue: / notNil ifTrue:.
- No mid-block | var | declarations.
- Use Array new: 0 not Array new.
- Use copyWith: not add:. Use reject: not remove:.
- Use at:ifAbsent: with ifAbsent block where needed.
- Arrays are 1-based. Strings are 1-based with charCode method.
- CellBuffer coordinates are 0-based.

REFERENCE FILES:
- src/widgets/Window.mag (Window structure)
- src/widgets/ListView.mag (scrolling event handling pattern)
- src/mvc/View.mag (base class)
- src/display/CellBuffer.mag (putChar, putString API)
- src/display/Display.mag (addWindow:, removeWindow:, run)

Update src/Main.mag to create Display, open a Workspace and Transcript, and run the display event loop.

File: src/Main.mag (MODIFY existing file, do not create new)
Namespace: Alto, Import: Go.

Replace the existing Main>>start method entirely. The current implementation manually draws a box and waits for a single keypress. Replace with:

Main subclass: Object
  classMethod: start [
    | display |
    display := Display new.
    Workspace open: display.
    Transcript open: display.
    Transcript show: 'Alto started.'.
    display run
  ]

KEY POINTS:
- Remove ALL the old manual box-drawing code (lines 12-76 of current Main.mag).
- The method should be very simple: create display, open tools, run event loop.
- ALL temp vars at top of method (just | display | in this case).
- No ifNil:/ifNotNil:, no mid-block temp vars.
- Keep namespace: 'Alto' and import: 'Go' header.
- Keep the class comment (update it to reflect new purpose).

The Display>>run method already handles the event loop (render, pollEvent, dispatch to top window). The Workspace and Transcript open: methods add windows to the display. So Main just needs to wire them together.

VERIFICATION: After editing, the file should:
1. Have namespace/import header
2. Define Main subclass: Object
3. Have a single classMethod: start that creates Display, opens Workspace and Transcript, shows a startup message, and calls display run.
4. Be under 15 lines of actual code.

REFERENCE: Current src/Main.mag (to understand what to replace), src/display/Display.mag (Display new, run).

Create test/test_phase5.mag — unit tests for Workspace and Transcript tools.

Create a test file following the pattern established in test/test_phase4b.mag (test runner with assert:message: and assertEqual:to:message: methods, plus a report method).

Tests to write:

WORKSPACE TESTS:
- WS1: Workspace new creates instance with textLines as empty array (Array new: 0), cursorX = 0, cursorY = 0, scrollOffset = 0.
- WS2: Workspace is a subclass of View (responds to invalidate, displayOn:, etc.).
- WS3: After inserting a printable char event, textLines has one line with that char, cursorX = 1.
- WS4: After typing multiple chars, the line contains all chars in order.
- WS5: Enter key splits line and moves cursor to next line. Type 'ab', press Enter, type 'cd'. textLines should have 'ab' and 'cd'. cursorY = 1, cursorX = 2.
- WS6: Backspace at start of non-first line merges with previous line.
- WS7: Backspace in middle of line removes character and decrements cursorX.
- WS8: Arrow up/down move cursorY within bounds. Up at 0 stays 0. Down past last line stays at last line.
- WS9: Arrow left/right move cursorX within bounds.
- WS10: Backspace at position 0 of line 0 does nothing.

TRANSCRIPT TESTS:
- TR1: Transcript new creates instance with empty lines array, maxLines = 1000, scrollOffset = 0.
- TR2: Transcript current returns singleton (same object on repeated calls).
- TR3: Transcript show: aString appends to lines array.
- TR4: After show: 'hello', lines size = 1, lines at: 1 = 'hello' (1-based).
- TR5: Multiple show: calls append in order.
- TR6: Transcript is read-only — handleEvent: with printable char does NOT modify lines.
- TR7: Up/down events change scrollOffset.
- TR8: scrollOffset does not go below 0.
- TR9: Transcript is a subclass of View.

NOTE ON EVENT SIMULATION: Events in this system come from tcell (Go terminal library). For unit testing, we need to create mock event objects. Check how events are used in the handlers — they appear to be dictionaries or Go objects. For testing, create Dictionary objects with appropriate keys (#key, #rune, etc.) that match what the handlers expect. Look at how ListView.handleEvent: uses evt (evt at: #key) to understand the expected event format.

CONVENTIONS:
- Use the same test runner pattern as test/test_phase4b.mag.
- ALL temp vars at top of method.
- Use copyWith: not add:. Use Array new: 0.
- Print results clearly with test IDs (WS1, WS2, TR1, etc.).

Run the tests and report results. Note any failures that indicate bugs in the Workspace or Transcript implementations.

Review all Phase 5 files for correctness, convention compliance, and integration.

Files to review:
1. src/tools/Workspace.mag
2. src/tools/Transcript.mag
3. src/Main.mag (updated)
4. test/test_phase5.mag

REVIEW CHECKLIST:

Syntax & Convention Compliance:
- [ ] namespace: 'Alto', import: 'Go' header on all .mag files
- [ ] ALL temp vars declared at top of each method in single | ... | block
- [ ] NO mid-block | var | declarations anywhere
- [ ] NO ifNil:/ifNotNil: — must use isNil ifTrue: / notNil ifTrue:
- [ ] Integer char codes used (not dollar-sign literals like $A)
- [ ] Array new: 0 used (not Array new)
- [ ] copyWith: used for array growth (not add:)
- [ ] reject: used for array removal (not remove: or removeAt:)
- [ ] at:ifAbsent: used with block (not at: alone for unsafe access)
- [ ] instanceVars: keyword form (not bracket-delimited class body)
- [ ] subclass: View (not subclass: Object) for both Workspace and Transcript
- [ ] classMethod: new follows standard pattern (super new, initialize, ^instance)

Workspace Correctness:
- [ ] Handles printable chars, Enter, Backspace, arrow keys, Escape
- [ ] Cursor position updated correctly for each operation
- [ ] Text line splitting on Enter is correct (0-based vs 1-based indexing)
- [ ] Backspace at line start merges with previous line
- [ ] displayOn: renders text and cursor (reverse-video for cursor position)
- [ ] scrollOffset updates when cursor moves out of visible area
- [ ] open: creates Window, adds to Display, wires up controller

Transcript Correctness:
- [ ] Singleton pattern works (current returns same instance)
- [ ] show: appends line and auto-scrolls to bottom
- [ ] maxLines trimming works (keeps most recent 1000 lines)
- [ ] Read-only: handleEvent: only handles scroll and Escape
- [ ] displayOn: renders lines from scrollOffset, newest at bottom
- [ ] open: creates Window, adds to Display

Integration:
- [ ] Main.mag creates Display, opens both tools, runs event loop
- [ ] Window/Display integration: windows added correctly, events dispatched
- [ ] Workspace and Transcript can coexist (different frame positions)

Known Codebase Issues (do NOT flag these as new bugs):
- CellBuffer has a known charCode bug (Dictionary>>at:put: not understood in putChar:x:y:style:)
- ListView has known off-by-one bugs (items at: index + 1)
- Window contentBuf not composited back (content may not render)
- These are pre-existing; do not count against Phase 5 review.

Report: List all issues found with severity (critical/moderate/minor). Critical = will cause runtime error or wrong behavior. Moderate = convention violation or edge case bug. Minor = style/clarity issue.
